name: Lutheran Social Service Of Minnesota
description_short: NFCC-certified nonprofit financial counseling service offering free, confidential budget, debt management, and housing counseling for over 35 years.
description_long:
Lutheran Social Service of Minnesota (LSS) is a certified nonprofit financial counseling organization that has operated for more than 35 years. The organization is accredited by the National Foundation for Credit Counseling (NFCC) and provides trusted, nonjudgmental financial guidance to consumers working toward financial wellness.

LSS offers a comprehensive suite of financial counseling services including consumer credit counseling, budget and debt management counseling, student loan debt repayment counseling, debt management plans, homeownership counseling, and foreclosure prevention counseling. Their financial counselors provide realistic, action-oriented, and confidential support to help clients develop healthy financial habits, pay off debt, and achieve financial stability. The organization also offers Financial Choice, a tailored financial wellness service for businesses and organizations.

LSS distinguishes itself through its NFCC certification, indicating counselors meet professional standards and ethical guidelines. The organization emphasizes nonjudgmental support and has developed a reputation that clients describe as providing a "lifeboat" during financial crisis. Their multilingual website (40+ languages) reflects commitment to serving diverse Minnesota communities.

As a free nonprofit service, LSS has no financial incentive to steer clients toward expensive solutions. However, the website provides limited detail on counselor qualifications beyond NFCC certification, typical session duration, or specific success metrics. Availability may be constrained by nonprofit funding, and services appear Minnesota-focused despite the national NFCC affiliation.
pros: NFCC-certified financial counselors meeting professional standards
Completely free and confidential counseling services
Offers nonprofit debt management plans as alternative to commercial debt relief
Specialized student loan debt repayment counseling and options guidance
Foreclosure prevention and reverse mortgage counseling for homeowners
Multilingual support (40+ languages) for diverse populations
35+ year track record as established nonprofit organization
cons: Website lacks detail on average counselor qualifications, experience levels, or specializations
No information on average session length, frequency, or typical engagement duration
Services appear Minnesota-focused; unclear if available nationwide despite NFCC affiliation
No published success rates, client outcomes data, or measurable results metrics
Limited details on debt management plan terms, creditor participation rates, or typical payoff timelines
best_for: Minnesota residents with high-interest debt seeking free budget and debt management counseling
Homeowners facing foreclosure or interested in reverse mortgage options
Recent graduates and current students struggling with federal student loan repayment decisions
diagnosis: Best for Minnesota residents seeking free, nonjudgmental debt and budget counseling from a certified nonprofit with no financial incentive to recommend expensive solutions. Main caveat: website provides limited transparency on counselor backgrounds, program outcomes, and geographic service area beyond Minnesota.
services: Debt payoff planning and action plans
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 709 University Avenue W, Saint Paul, MN 55104
